fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: District of Columbia Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to District of Columbia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity district of columbia, how to transfer physical therapist license to district of columbia, physical therapist compact district of columbia, district of columbia physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: DC Board of Physical Therapy
board.parent_dept: DC Department of Health, Health Regulation and Licensing Administration (HRLA)
board.mailing_address:
DC Health
Health Regulation and Licensing Administration
Board of Physical Therapy
899 North Capitol Street NE, 3rd Floor
Washington, DC 20002

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in District of Columbia.

steps.title: Create Account on DC Health Licensing Portal
steps.description: Register for a new user account on the DC Health Licensing Portal at hrla.doh.dc.gov/hrla/default.asp if you do not already have one. You will need to verify your email address during this process.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your newly created or existing account, select the 'Physical Therapist' application type (either 'By Examination' for new graduates or 'By Endorsement' for out-of-state licensees), and accurately complete all required sections of the application form.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Upload official transcripts verifying completion of an accredited physical therapy program. Arrange for official NPTE scores to be sent directly to the Board. If applying by endorsement, provide verification of licensure from all other states or jurisdictions where you have held a PT license.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check (CBC)
steps.description: Follow the specific instructions provided within the DC Health Licensing Portal or by the Board of Physical Therapy to complete the mandatory criminal background check.

steps.title: Pay Application Fees
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $200 (for both initial and endorsement applications) and any other associated fees through the DC Health Licensing Portal using a credit/debit card.

steps.title: Board Review and Licensure
steps.description: The Board of Physical Therapy will review your complete application and all supporting documentation. Upon approval and verification that all requirements are met, your Physical Therapist license will be issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a District of Columbia physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $200. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does District of Columbia have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers physical therapist practice in District of Columbia. To work in District of Columbia, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to District of Columbia?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in District of Columbia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in District of Columbia
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in the District of Columbia, navigate to the DC Health Licensing Portal and begin your application.

official_links.registration_steps: On the DC Health Licensing Portal, click 'Register for an account' or 'New User Registration'. Follow the prompts to create your user account, verify your email, and then log in to start your application.
